mnemonics: H.I.P.P.O. Hospitals (Human & Animal), Industries, Producers (Agricultural), Practices (Research & Labs), Others (Home/Offices)
keyPoints: Major sources include hospitals, clinics, and veterinary centers generating infectious waste.
Industries, particularly pharmaceutical and biotechnology, produce chemical and discarded medicine waste.
Smaller but significant sources include research laboratories, agricultural farms, and even households.
commonMistakes: Assuming biomedical waste is generated only by large hospitals and ignoring smaller sources like clinics, labs, and homes.
Believing all waste from a healthcare facility is hazardous; a significant portion is general municipal solid waste.
Confusing e-waste (electronic) with biomedical waste, which is biologically or chemically hazardous from medical activities.
